name: passencore_seg_disagree
description: Segmenter-disagreement experiment. The Wake portmanteau `passencore` (003.04) is decomposed differently by the 4 unsupervised segmenters trained on the Wake (BPE-4k, Unigram-4k, Morfessor, FlatCat). Each morpheme gets a POS tag from Stanza's English tagger (treating it as a standalone word — noisy by construction, but algorithmic). We train each unique morpheme as a single-morpheme word with its tagger-assigned POS, then probe `passencore` four times — once per segmenter — and compare the resulting N/V ratios. The spread across segmenters is the experimental result.

pos_to_bucket._other: untrained — PHON block exists but never receives N/V supervision; contributes ~0 to both hubs

caveat: Morpheme POS tags are produced by Stanza's English tagger fed each morpheme as a standalone token. Per the artifact docstring, this is low-confidence (word-shape heuristics). `en` tagged PROPN is a particular case — the tagger likely capitalizes its expectation; in context it's the French preposition or the affix. Treating PROPN → noun follows our prior aggregator convention.

untrained_morphemes_in_inventory.reason: tagged INTJ by unigram-4000; no N/V mapping. Block exists in PHON inventory so unigram's probe can fire it, but it receives no supervision.

probe_words.prediction: N/V > 1 but slightly less than bpe (1 V + 2 N + 1 noise morpheme); `re`'s noise should pull magnitudes up slightly without changing ratio direction
